Off Calendar Dec-17-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. CARRIE RICHEY of Cooley LLP, Tel No. (650) 843-5867 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injuction is granted. The court enjoins defendant and his agents and others acting in concert with him from selling or offering to sell plaintiff's artwork or representing plaintiff in any capacity. Defendant does not oppose this relief. The court further enjoins defendant from transferring, encumbering, selling or hypothecating any part or the whole of real property located at 866 Geary Street. Plaintiff demonstrates a likelihood of prevailing on his breach of trust and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action. The evidence demonstrates that defendant failed to turn over all funds that were owed to plaintiff and used those funds to purchase the building. Plaintiff shall post a $20,000 undertaking per CCP 529. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)
